title: hieracium sonchoides subsp. sonchoides
intro: Hieracium sonchoides subsp. sonchoides, commonly known as Hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family, with yellow daisy-like flowers and a bitter, milky sap. The medicinal properties of Hawkweed include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Hawkweed include its ability to stimulate the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Hawkweed is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, and can be consumed as a tea or applied topically to affected areas.
